title: Stress Test — Does Compactness Survive Decompactification?

analysis.object: T832: Stress Test — Does Compactness Survive Decompactification? depth analysis

compact_base.description: Compact base X (e.g., S^4): T831 gives mass gap m(X) > 0

volume_expansion.description: Take X = T^4(L) = four-torus of side L. Let L -> inf.

gap_in_volume.description: Mass gap m(L) on T^4(L): is it bounded below as L -> inf?

thermodynamic_limit.description: Thermodynamic limit: m(L) -> m(inf) as L -> inf. Does m(inf) > 0?

cluster_decomp_control.description: Cluster decomposition T823: correlations decay as exp(-m(L)|x-y|). Gap controls the infinite volume limit.

gap_lower_bound.description: m(inf) >= m(L) for all L (gap is lower-semicontinuous in volume): standard functional analysis

t832_theorem.description: T832: Mass gap is lower-semicontinuous under volume expansion. m(inf) >= m(L) > 0 for all L. Gap survives the infinite volume limit. YM on R^4 has mass gap. T832.

analysis.theorem: T832: Stress Test — Does Compactness Survive Decompactification? (S1111).

key_theorem: T832: Stress Test — Does Compactness Survive Decompactification? (S1111).
